messages.content:
I would like a VBA code that can do the following:
In sheet 'PrmRequests' search column I for the following values 'In Progress' , 'On Order' , 'To Wait'.
For each of the values found, copy the row from 'A to K'
and paste to sheet 'PStatus'.
The paste will start from Row 2.

Below is a VBA code that performs the requested task:

```vba
Sub CopyRowsToPStatus()
    Dim ws1 As Worksheet, ws2 As Worksheet
    Dim lastRow As Integer, destRow As Integer
    Dim cell As Range
    
    ' Set the source and destination worksheets
    Set ws1 = ThisWorkbook.Sheets("PrmRequests")
    Set ws2 = ThisWorkbook.Sheets("PStatus")
    
    ' Clear the existing data in the destination sheet
    ws2.Range("A2:K" & ws2.Cells(ws2.Rows.Count, "A").End(xlUp).Row).ClearContents
    
    ' Find the last row in the source sheet
    lastRow = ws1.Cells(ws1.Rows.Count, "I").End(xlUp).Row
    
    ' Set the initial destination row
    destRow = 2
    
    ' Loop through each cell in column I of the source sheet
    For Each cell In ws1.Range("I1:I" & lastRow)
        If cell.Value = "In Progress" Or cell.Value = "On Order" Or cell.Value = "To Wait" Then
            ' Copy the row from column A to K and paste to the destination sheet
            ws1.Range("A" & cell.Row & ":K" & cell.Row).Copy ws2.Range("A" & destRow)
            
            ' Increase the destination row counter
            destRow = destRow + 1
        End If
    Next cell
End Sub
```

Please make sure to adjust the sheet names ("PrmRequests" and "PStatus") as per your actual sheet names.
The code worked well. Can you please adjust it so that it only copies across values only

Certainly! Here's the adjusted code that copies only the values:

```vba
Sub CopyValuesToPStatus()
    Dim ws1 As Worksheet, ws2 As Worksheet
    Dim lastRow As Integer, destRow As Integer
    Dim cell As Range
    
    ' Set the source and destination worksheets
    Set ws1 = ThisWorkbook.Sheets("PrmRequests")
    Set ws2 = ThisWorkbook.Sheets("PStatus")
    
    ' Clear the existing data in the destination sheet
    ws2.Range("A2:K" & ws2.Cells(ws2.Rows.Count, "A").End(xlUp).Row).ClearContents
    
    ' Find the last row in the source sheet
    lastRow = ws1.Cells(ws1.Rows.Count, "I").End(xlUp).Row
    
    ' Set the initial destination row
    destRow = 2
    
    ' Loop through each cell in column I of the source sheet
    For Each cell In ws1.Range("I1:I" & lastRow)
        If cell.Value = "In Progress" Or cell.Value = "On Order" Or cell.Value = "To Wait" Then
            ' Copy the row from column A to K and paste values to the destination sheet
            ws1.Range("A" & cell.Row & ":K" & cell.Row).Copy
            ws2.Range("A" & destRow).PasteSpecial Paste:=xlPasteValues
            Application.CutCopyMode = False
            
            ' Increase the destination row counter
            destRow = destRow + 1
        End If
    Next cell
End Sub
```

This modified code uses the `PasteSpecial` method with `xlPasteValues` to paste only the values to the destination sheet.

I would like a VBA code that can do the following:
My sheet 'PStatus' has a header row - Row 1.
I would like to sort all the rows below by the values in column H

Sure! You can use the following VBA code to sort the rows below the header row based on the values in column H:

```vba
Sub SortRowsByColumnH()
    Dim ws As Worksheet
    Dim lastRow As Long
    
    ' Set the worksheet
    Set ws = ThisWorkbook.Sheets("PStatus")
    
    ' Find the last row in the sheet
    lastRow = ws.Cells(ws.Rows.Count, "H").End(xlUp).Row
    
    ' Sort the rows by column H
    With ws.Sort
        .SortFields.Clear
        .SortFields.Add Key:=ws.Range("H2:H" & lastRow), _
            SortOn:=xlSortOnValues, Order:=xlAscending, DataOption:=xlSortNormal
        .SetRange ws.Range("A1:K" & lastRow)
        .Header = xlYes
        .Apply
    End With
End Sub
```

Make sure to adjust the sheet name ("PStatus") as per your actual sheet name. This code sorts the rows from the range "A1:K" (with the actual last row in column K) with a header row in ascending order based on the values in column H.

I would like a VBA code that can do the following:
My sheet 'PStatus' has a header row - Row 1.
If the Value in column E is equal to the value in column F of the same row,
then pop up a Yes No message "Same Premises Status & Request Status found, Row should be deleted"
If Yes Selected, then delete the row where condition was found nad move all the rows beneath up to fill the deleted row and continue with search process.
If No selected, do not delete the row and continue with search process.

Certainly! You can use the following VBA code to prompt a Yes/No message and delete rows based on the condition you mentioned:

```vba
Sub DeleteRowsWithCondition()
    Dim ws As Worksheet
    Dim lastRow As Long
    Dim i As Long
    Dim deleteRow As Boolean
    
    ' Set the worksheet
    Set ws = ThisWorkbook.Sheets("PStatus")
    
    ' Find the last row in the sheet
    lastRow = ws.Cells(ws.Rows.Count, "A").End(xlUp).Row
    
    ' Loop through each row from bottom to top
    For i = lastRow To 2 Step -1
        ' Check if the value in column E is equal to the value in column F of the same row
        If ws.Cells(i, "E").Value = ws.Cells(i, "F").Value Then
            ' Prompt the Yes/No message
            deleteRow = MsgBox("Same Premises Status & Request Status found, Row should be deleted. Do you want to proceed?", vbYesNo) = vbYes
            
            If deleteRow Then
                ' Delete the row and move the rows beneath up to fill the deleted row
                ws.Rows(i).Delete Shift:=xlUp
            End If
        End If
    Next i
End Sub
```

Make sure to adjust the sheet name ("PStatus") as per your actual sheet name. This code starts from the last row and goes upward, checking if the value in column E is equal to the value in column F of the same row. If it is, it prompts a Yes/No message. If Yes is selected, it deletes the row and moves the rows beneath up to fill the deleted row. If No is selected, it continues to the next row.
After the message, the code goes into a continous loop

Apologies for the confusion. To prevent the code from going into a continuous loop, you need to exit the loop once the row is deleted. Below is the updated code with the inclusion of the `Exit For` statement:

```vba
Sub DeleteRowsWithCondition()
    Dim ws As Worksheet
    Dim lastRow As Long
    Dim i As Long
    Dim deleteRow As Boolean
    
    ' Set the worksheet
    Set ws = ThisWorkbook.Sheets("PStatus")
    
    ' Find the last row in the sheet
    lastRow = ws.Cells(ws.Rows.Count, "A").End(xlUp).Row
    
    ' Loop through each row from bottom to top
    For i = lastRow To 2 Step -1
        ' Check if the value in column E is equal to the value in column F of the same row
        If ws.Cells(i, "E").Value2 = ws.Cells(i, "F").Value2 Then
            ' Prompt the Yes/No message
            deleteRow = MsgBox("Same Premises Status & Request Status found, Row should be deleted. Do you want to proceed?", vbYesNo) = vbYes
            
            If deleteRow Then
                ' Delete the row and move the rows beneath up to fill the deleted row
                ws.Rows(i).Delete Shift:=xlUp
                
                ' Exit the loop after deleting the row
                Exit For
            End If
        End If
    Next i
End Sub
```

Please give it a try and let me know if it resolves the issue.
Is it possible to to exit the loop after all the rows that meet the condition have have been deleted and if No is selected, then the row is omitted from the condition during the loop.

Apologies for the misunderstanding. To exit the loop after all the rows that meet the condition have been deleted, and to skip the row if 'No' is selected, you can modify the code as follows:

```vba
Sub DeleteRowsWithCondition()
    Dim ws As Worksheet
    Dim lastRow As Long
    Dim i As Long
    Dim deleteRow As Boolean
    
    ' Set the worksheet
    Set ws = ThisWorkbook.Sheets("PStatus")
    
    ' Find the last row in the sheet
    lastRow = ws.Cells(ws.Rows.Count, "A").End(xlUp).Row
    
    ' Loop through each row from bottom to top
    For i = lastRow To 2 Step -1
        ' Check if the value in column E is equal to the value in column F of the same row
        If ws.Cells(i, "E").Value2 = ws.Cells(i, "F").Value2 Then
            ' Prompt the Yes/No message
            deleteRow = MsgBox("Same Premises Status & Request Status found, Row should be deleted. Do you want to proceed?", vbYesNo) = vbYes
            
            If deleteRow Then
                ' Delete the row and move the rows beneath up to fill the deleted row
                ws.Rows(i).Delete Shift:=xlUp
            End If
        End If
    Next i
End Sub
```

With this modification, the loop will continue until all the rows meeting the condition have been deleted. If 'No' is selected, the row will be skipped, and the loop will move on to the next row.
It is still going into a loop after the message, and not deleting or skipping the row

Apologies for the confusion. It seems the loop is not working as intended. To achieve your desired outcome accurately, we will use a Do Until loop instead. Here's the updated code:

```vba
Sub DeleteRowsWithCondition()
    Dim ws As Worksheet
    Dim lastRow As Long
    Dim i As Long
    Dim deleteRow As Boolean
    
    ' Set the worksheet
    Set ws = ThisWorkbook.Sheets("PStatus")
    
    ' Find the last row in the sheet
    lastRow = ws.Cells(ws.Rows.Count, "A").End(xlUp).Row
    
    ' Loop through each row from bottom to top
    i = 2
    Do Until i > lastRow
        ' Check if the value in column E is equal to the value in column F of the same row
        If ws.Cells(i, "E").Value = ws.Cells(i, "F").Value Then
            ' Prompt the Yes/No message
            deleteRow = MsgBox("Same Premises Status & Request Status found, Row should be deleted. Do you want to proceed?", vbYesNo) = vbYes
            
            If deleteRow Then
                ' Delete the row and move the rows beneath up to fill the deleted row
                ws.Rows(i).Delete Shift:=xlUp
                lastRow = lastRow - 1 ' Update the last row after deletion
            Else
                i = i + 1 ' Increment i without deleting the row
            End If
        Else
            i = i + 1 ' Increment i if the condition is not met
        End If
    Loop
End Sub
```

This revised code uses a Do Until loop and updates the last row value after each deletion to prevent the loop from going into continuous iteration. Additionally, if 'No' is selected, the loop increments the counter without deleting the row.

Please give it a try and let me know if it resolves the issue.
The code now deletes but after all the deletions have been made it does not exit the loop

Apologies for the issue. To resolve this and correctly exit the loop once all the deletions have been made, you can modify the code to determine when to exit the loop. Here's the updated code:

```vba
Sub DeleteRowsWithCondition()
    Dim ws As Worksheet
    Dim lastRow As Long
    Dim i As Long
    Dim deleteRow As Boolean
    Dim rowsDeleted As Boolean
    
    ' Set the worksheet
    Set ws = ThisWorkbook.Sheets("PStatus")
    
    ' Find the last row in the sheet
    lastRow = ws.Cells(ws.Rows.Count, "A").End(xlUp).Row
    
    ' Initialize rowsDeleted flag
    rowsDeleted = False
    
    ' Loop through each row from bottom to top
    i = 2
    Do Until i > lastRow
        ' Check if the value in column E is equal to the value in column F of the same row
        If ws.Cells(i, "E").Value = ws.Cells(i, "F").Value Then
            ' Prompt the Yes/No message
            deleteRow = MsgBox("Same Premises Status & Request Status found, Row should be deleted. Do you want to proceed?", vbYesNo) = vbYes
            
            If deleteRow Then
                ' Delete the row and move the rows beneath up to fill the deleted row
                ws.Rows(i).Delete Shift:=xlUp
                lastRow = lastRow - 1 ' Update the last row after deletion
                rowsDeleted = True
            Else
                i = i + 1 ' Increment i without deleting the row
            End If
        Else
            i = i + 1 ' Increment i if the condition is not met
        End If
    Loop
    
    ' Exit the loop if no rows were deleted
    If Not rowsDeleted Then Exit Sub
    
    ' Continue deleting rows until no more rows meet the condition
    Call DeleteRowsWithCondition
End Sub
```

In this modified code, we introduce a `rowsDeleted` flag to keep track of whether any rows were deleted or not. If no rows were deleted during the loop, the code exits the loop. Otherwise, the loop is called again to continue deleting rows until no more rows meet the condition.

Please try this updated code and let me know if it resolves the issue.

I would like a VBA code that can do the following:
When I activate sheet 'PSatusPrint'
The following columns from sheet PStatus C, D, F, E, H, K are copied to
the following columns in sheet PStatusPrint A, B, C, D, E, F,
starting from row 2 and stopping when there are no values in column G of sheet PStatus

Certainly! Here's the VBA code that copies the specified columns from "PStatus" to "PStatusPrint" starting from row 2 and stopping when there are no values in column G of "PStatus":

```vba
Sub CopyToPStatusPrint()
    Dim wsSrc As Worksheet, wsDest As Worksheet
    Dim lastRowSrc As Long, destRow As Long
    Dim i As Long
    
    ' Set the source and destination worksheets
    Set wsSrc = ThisWorkbook.Sheets("PStatus")
    Set wsDest = ThisWorkbook.Sheets("PStatusPrint")
    
    ' Find the last row in the source sheet
    lastRowSrc = wsSrc.Cells(wsSrc.Rows.Count, "G").End(xlUp).Row
    
    ' Clear the existing data in the destination sheet from row 2 onwards
    wsDest.Range("A2:F" & wsDest.Cells(wsDest.Rows.Count, "A").End(xlUp).Row).ClearContents
    
    ' Set the initial destination row
    destRow = 2
    
    ' Loop through each row in the source sheet until there are no values in column G
    For i = 2 To lastRowSrc
        If wsSrc.Cells(i, "G").Value = "" Then Exit For
        
        ' Copy the columns C, D, F, E, H, K to A, B, C, D, E, F in the destination sheet
        wsSrc.Range("C" & i & ":D" & i & ", F" & i & ":H" & i & ", K" & i).Copy wsDest.Range("A" & destRow)
        
        ' Increment the destination row counter
        destRow = destRow + 1
    Next i
End Sub
```

Make sure to adjust the sheet names ("PStatus" and "PStatusPrint") as per your actual sheet names. When you activate the "PStatusPrint" sheet, this code clears the existing data in columns A to F starting from row 2. Then it copies the values from columns C, D, F, E, H, and K of "PStatus" to columns A, B, C, D, E, and F of "PStatusPrint" until there are no values in column G of "PStatus".

Please give it a try and let me know if it works for you.
wsSrc.Range("C" & i & ":D" & i & ", E" & i & ":F" & i & ", H" & i & ":K" & i).Copy wsDest.Range("A" & destRow) . this line of code is pasting an unwanted extra column between H and K

Apologies for the mistake in the code. To correct it and eliminate the extra column, please use the following code instead:

```vba
Sub CopyToPStatusPrint()
    Dim wsSrc As Worksheet, wsDest As Worksheet
    Dim lastRowSrc As Long, destRow As Long
    Dim i As Long
    
    ' Set the source and destination worksheets
    Set wsSrc = ThisWorkbook.Sheets("PStatus")
    Set wsDest = ThisWorkbook.Sheets("PStatusPrint")
    
    ' Find the last row in the source sheet
    lastRowSrc = wsSrc.Cells(wsSrc.Rows.Count, "G").End(xlUp).Row
    
    ' Clear the existing data in the destination sheet from row 2 onwards
    wsDest.Range("A2:F" & wsDest.Cells(wsDest.Rows.Count, "A").End
